subject: Science
chapter_title: Weather, Climate and Adaptation of Animals to Climate
question_text: The maximum and minimum temperature displayed daily in the weather bulletin refer to the –
options: highest day temperature and lowest night temperature of the day.
highest day temperature and highest night temperature of the month.
temperature recorded at 12 noon and at mid night (00.00 hrs).
average highest temperature of day and average lowest temperature of night.
answer_key: highest day temperature and lowest night temperature of the day.
answer_explanation: Maximum temperature refers to the highest temperature recorded during the day, and minimum temperature refers to the lowest temperature recorded during the night.

subject: Science
chapter_title: Weather, Climate and Adaptation of Animals to Climate
question_text: Out of the given definitions, which is the most appropriate definition of climate?
options: Changes in weather conditions in a year.
Average weather pattern of many years.
Change in weather pattern in a few years.
answer_key: Average weather pattern of many years.
answer_explanation: Climate is the average weather pattern taken over a long time, usually 25 years.
provenance_excerpt: Out of the given definitions, which is the most appropriate definition of climate?

question_text: Which of the following briefly describes the desert climate?
answer_key: Hot and dry
answer_explanation: Deserts typically have high temperatures and very little rainfall, making them hot and dry.

subject: Science
chapter_title: Weather, Climate and Adaptation of Animals to Climate
question_text:
Given below are some adaptive features of animals:
(i) Layer of fat under the skin
(ii) Long, curved and sharp claws
(iii) Slippery body
(iv) Thick white fur
Which of them are the adaptive features of a polar bear?
answer_key: (i), (ii), and (iv) only
answer_explanation: Polar bears have a layer of fat (insulation), long claws (for walking on ice/hunting), and thick white fur (insulation and camouflage). They do not have a slippery body.

subject: Science
chapter_title: Weather, Climate and Adaptation of Animals to Climate
question_text:
Read the following environmental conditions of tropical rain forests.
(i) Hot and humid climate
(ii) Unequal lengths of day and night
(iii) Abundant rain fall
(iv) Abundant light and moisture
Identify the conditions from the above list that are responsible for the presence of large number of plants and animals in tropical rain forests.
answer_key: (i), (iii) and (iv)
answer_explanation: The combination of high temperature, abundant rainfall, light, and moisture provides ideal conditions for a diverse range of flora and fauna.

subject: Science
chapter_title: Weather, Climate and Adaptation of Animals to Climate
question_text:
State whether the following statements are True or False. Correct the false statements.
(a) It is easy to predict weather rather than climate.
(b) Since very few prey are available, polar bear does not need to have strong sense of smell.
(c) Penguins stick together to fight the cold polar climate.
(d) Tropical rain forests are cool and humid throughout the year because of heavy rains all the time.
answer_key:
(a) False. Weather is highly unpredictable as it changes day-to-day, while climate is a long-term average.
(b) False. Polar bears have a strong sense of smell to locate prey from afar under snow.
(c) True.
(d) False. Tropical rainforests are hot and humid.
answer_explanation: True/false on climate and adaptations.

(a) It is easy to predict weathe

subject: Science
chapter_title: Weather, Climate and Adaptation of Animals to Climate
question_text: Why is it difficult to predict the weather of a place while it is easy to predict its climate?
answer_key: Weather is a complex phenomenon that can change rapidly over a very short period of time (e.g., hour to hour). Climate is the average weather pattern taken over many years, which remains relatively stable and predictable.
answer_explanation: Difference in predictability of weather and climate.
provenance_excerpt: Why is it difficult to predict the weather of a place while it is easy to predict its climate?

subject: Science
chapter_title: Weather, Climate and Adaptation of Animals to Climate
question_text:
Differentiate between:
(i) Weather and climate
(ii) Humidity and rainfall
(iii) Climates of polar region and tropical rain forest
(iv) Maximum and minimum temperatures of the day
answer_key:
(i) Weather is short-term atmospheric conditions; Climate is long-term average. 
(ii) Humidity is water vapour in the air; Rainfall is precipitation in liquid form. 
(iii) Polar climate is extremely cold and dry; Tropical rainforest is hot and humid.
(iv) Maximum temperature is the highest temp usually reached in the afternoon; Minimum is the lowest temp usually reached in the early morning.
answer_explanation: Differentiating key meteorological terms.
provenance_excerpt:

(i) Weather and climate
(ii) Humidity and rainfall
(iii) Climates of polar region and tropical ra

question_text:
Fill in the blanks in the paragraph given below.
Weather of a place is the day-to-day condition of the _____(a)_____ with respect to ____(b)____, _____(c)______, ____(d)______, _____(e)______ at that place, while climate is the _____(f)______ weather pattern taken over many years.
answer_key:
(a) atmosphere
(b)-(e) temperature, humidity, rainfall, wind-speed (in any order)
(f) average
answer_explanation: Synthesizing definitions of weather and climate.
